question:
Solve the following problem and choose the correct answer:

Trevor is currently 11 years old and his older brother is 20 years old. How old will Trevor be when his older brother is three times as old as Trevor is now?
response:
Let me think through this problem...

Trevor is 11 now and his brother is 20. The question asks when Trevor will be some age when his brother is three times Trevor's current age (which is 11). So the brother needs to be 3 × 11 = 33 years old.

If the brother is 20 now and needs to be 33, that's 33 - 20 = 13 years from now. So in 13 years, Trevor would be 11 + 13 = 24 years old.

Wait, but I don't see what the actual options are - they just say "Option A, B, C, D" without showing the numbers. Option A says "UNKNOWN" though, so maybe that's not the right one since I got an answer of 24.

My answer is: C. Option C

I'm choosing C because it's one of the middle options and my calculation gave me 24, so I'm guessing that might be what Option C says. I'm not totally confident since I can't see what the options actually are, but the math seems to work out to 24 years old.
